titel: Supplementary provisions regarding more flexible rules for payouts from pension insurance.
html:
The government, following an agreement within the Pensions Group, has submitted a proposal to the Parliament regarding more flexible rules for payouts from pension insurance, etc. (see prop. 2023/24:159, bet. 2024/25:SkU4, rskr. 2024/25:16, SFS 2024:974). The amendments to the law came into effect on January 1, 2025.

This proposal includes supplementary provisions to address certain outstanding issues that have been identified in the practical application of the new, more flexible payout rules. The proposals introduce a number of exceptions to the condition that payouts from a pension insurance must be made with the same or increasing amounts during the first five years (the five-year rule). According to the proposals, a pension can be paid out at a lower amount than the amount paid out in the immediately preceding payout if the reduction is due to changes in actuarial assumptions applied in calculating the payout amount, the deduction of fees stipulated in the insurance agreement, or changes in the number of payouts per year. The proposals apply to the payout of old-age pensions and survivor pensions from a pension insurance.

The proposal also includes a provision aimed at equalizing the conditions for fund-based and deposit-based insurance. The exception to the five-year rule that currently applies to fund-based insurance, which concerns adjustments to payouts due to changes in the value of fund units, is proposed to be extended to also apply to deposit-based insurance.

The new provisions are proposed to come into effect on January 1, 2026.

Proposed Parliamentary Decision

Prop. 2024/25:200

Government Proposal: The Riksdag (Parliament) shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Income Tax Act (1999:1229). 3

Prop. 2024/25:200 2 Proposed Act Amending the Income Tax Act (1999:1229)

Hereby, with respect to the Income Tax Act (1999:1229), the following shall apply:

  1. The current Section 58, Chapter 11a shall be renumbered as Section 58, Chapter 11b.
  2. Sections 58, Chapter 1a, 1b, the new Sections 11b and 15, and the heading immediately preceding Section 58, Chapter 15 shall have the following wording.
  3. Two new sections, 58, Chapter 11a and 14a, shall be introduced with the following wording.

Current Wording | Proposed Wording ---|--- 58, Chapter 1a | 58, Chapter 1a Such agreements regarding occupational pensions as referred to in Section 28, Chapter 2, second paragraph, shall: | Such agreements regarding occupational pensions as referred to in Section 28, Chapter 2, second paragraph, shall:

  1. be entered into with a foreign occupational pension institution, and | 1. be entered into with a foreign occupational pension institution, and
  2. comply with the conditions specified regarding pension insurance in: | 2. comply with the conditions specified regarding pension insurance in:
  • Sections 5 and 6 regarding the distinction between types of insurance, | * Sections 5 and 6 regarding the distinction between types of insurance,
  • Section 7 regarding occupational pension insurance, | * Section 7 regarding occupational pension insurance,
  • Sections 8 and 9, first and second paragraphs, regarding the insured person, | * Sections 8 and 9, first and second paragraphs, regarding the insured person,

| * Sections 10–14a regarding age, disability, and survivor pensions, and | * Sections 10–14a regarding age, disability, and survivor pensions, and

  • Section 15 regarding unit-linked insurance, and | * Section 15 regarding unit-linked insurance, and
  • Section 15 regarding insurance where the policyholders or other beneficiaries bear the investment risk, and | * Section 15 regarding insurance where the policyholders or other beneficiaries bear the investment risk, and
  • Sections 16–16b regarding the design of the insurance contract. | * Sections 16–16b regarding the design of the insurance contract.

What is meant by a foreign occupational pension institution is specified in Section 39, Chapter 13a. | What is meant by a foreign occupational pension institution is specified in Section 39, Chapter 13a. 58, Chapter 1b | 58, Chapter 1b For the application of Section 1a, the following shall apply: | For the application of Section 1a, the following shall apply:

  • The occupational pension institution shall be considered the insurer, | * The occupational pension institution shall be considered the insurer,
  • The employer shall be considered the policyholder, and | * The employer shall be considered the policyholder, and
  • The employee shall be considered the insured and beneficiary. | * The employee shall be considered the insured and beneficiary.

Regarding survivor pensions, according to Sections 13 and 14, the surviving spouse of the employee shall also be considered a beneficiary. | Regarding survivor pensions, according to Sections 13 and 14, the surviving spouse of the employee shall also be considered a beneficiary.

58, Chapter 11a | 58, Chapter 11a What is stated in Section 11 shall not preclude the payment of an old-age pension at a lower amount than the amount paid out at the immediately preceding payment, if the reduction is due to: | What is stated in Section 11 shall not preclude the payment of an old-age pension at a lower amount than the amount paid out at the immediately preceding payment, if the reduction is due to:

  1. changes in actuarial assumptions applied in the calculation of the payment amount, | 1. changes in actuarial assumptions applied in the calculation of the payment amount,
  2. charges that follow from the insurance contract, or | 2. charges that follow from the insurance contract, or
  3. changes in the number of payments per year. | 3. changes in the number of payments per year.

58, Chapter 11b | 58, Chapter 11b For an old-age pension according to a general pension plan that is to cease during the insured person's lifetime, instead of what is stated in Section 11, what is stated in the plan shall apply. What is meant by a general pension plan is specified in Section 20. | For an old-age pension according to a general pension plan that is to cease during the insured person's lifetime, instead of what is stated in Section 11, what is stated in the plan shall apply. What is meant by a general pension plan is specified in Section 20. 58, Chapter 14a | 58, Chapter 14a What is stated in Section 14 shall not preclude the payment of a survivor pension at a lower amount than the amount paid out at the immediately preceding payment, if the reduction is due to: | What is stated in Section 14 shall not preclude the payment of a survivor pension at a lower amount than the amount paid out at the immediately preceding payment, if the reduction is due to:

  1. changes in actuarial assumptions applied in the calculation of the payment amount, | 1. changes in actuarial assumptions applied in the calculation of the payment amount,
  2. charges that follow from the insurance contract, or | 2. charges that follow from the insurance contract, or
  3. changes in the number of payments per year. | 3. changes in the number of payments per year.

Unit-linked insurance | Insurance where the policyholders or other beneficiaries bear the investment risk 58, Chapter 15 | 58, Chapter 15 For unit-linked insurance class III | For pension insurance According to Section 2, Chapter 12 of the Insurance Business Act (2010:2043) or Section 2, Chapter 11 of the Act (2019:742) on occupational pension companies, | where the policyholders or other beneficiaries bear the investment risk, in the assessment according to Sections 11, 11a, | it shall apply to the assessment according to Sections 11, 11a,
tldr: The government is proposing new regulations regarding pension payouts. The aim is to make it easier for pension providers to pay out smaller amounts during the first five years, particularly if this is due to fees or changes in calculations.
lattlast:
The government has presented a proposal to the Parliament regarding payouts from pension insurance plans. The aim is to make the rules more flexible and to address problems that have arisen when the new rules have been applied in practice.

Currently, there is a rule known as the "five-year rule." This rule states that payouts from a pension insurance plan should not decrease during the first five years. The new proposal would allow for exceptions to this rule. This means that the payout may be reduced if the decrease is due to fees, changes in calculations, or if the frequency of payments is changed.

The proposal applies to both regular old-age pensions and survivor pensions, which are payments made to families when someone dies. The government also wants the rules to be the same for different types of insurance plans, such as both investment funds and managed accounts, when it comes to changes in the value of the funds chosen.

If the Parliament votes in favor of the proposal, the new rules will come into effect on January 1, 2026. The proposal involves a change to the Income Tax Act, which is the law that governs how taxes on wages and pensions are calculated.
